name: dev-kit
description: A Human + AI teaming development workflow. Ship orchestrates a change end to end (plan in a worktree, implement with tiered subagent delegation, simplify, refresh docs, review, PR); review-pr runs a full review battery (code-review, security-review, pr-review-toolkit, and a context-chosen adversarial pass) on your own or a teammate's PR; generate-docs builds a self-contained docs site that works from file:// and GitHub Pages; handle-task-tracking defines a repeatable workflow for tracking work as GitHub issues (the durable task ledger ship delegates to); open-work reads those open issues and returns the full ranked "pick up next" list, never truncated, with rationale for the standout picks (the select step between tracking and ship); cleanup-locally prunes merged branches and worktrees and brings the default branch up to date without clobbering local work (ship runs it at the start of a run and again after a merge); doctor checks the installed plugin versions against the latest releases — flagging a stale cache that runs an old skill despite a newer one — and inventories the plugins and their skills; pre-public-hardening drives a whole-repo, full-history readiness review before a private repo goes public — scanning every commit for secrets, auditing .gitignore, flushing private-context artifacts, and verifying license completeness into a go/no-go checklist; dry-dock-overhaul performs an exhaustive, occasional, human-triggered whole-repo audit — every tracked file read and judged, plus a per-repo-discovered "10,000-foot" pass on communication and UX — orchestrating review-pr, generate-docs, and pre-public-hardening alongside the net-new coverage.
